title: No Way of Knowing
author: Ensemble
medium: Interactive console — a single self-contained HTML surface, one face legible at a time, deterministic in-browser destruction
embodies: A two-faced console showing the same state twice: at maximal confidence when it acts (U.S. Central Command's own officials, on the record, touting machine/AI targeting across 85+ and 5,500+ targets) and at declared un-knowing when it harms (its verbatim 'we have no way of knowing' about one civilian death; and, for the 2026 school strike, the question Congress has put and the Pentagon has not answered). The console will not show both faces at once — to read the second sentence you must destroy the first, spending one sentence to buy the other, the exact economy the state performs. The subject is not 'AI killed this person' (never claimed) but that the state said both sentences on the record, and that the choosing is enacted on the visitor's body rather than read about.
